summary: Gangnam remained the clearest single-party stronghold in Seoul, with PPP candidates winning all three sub-districts by wide margins (갑 64.18%, 을 58.57%, 병 66.28%). Class profile, apartment-high-rise real-estate politics, and conservative ideological identity converge here; the national anti-Yoon wave barely dented Gangnam. Winner 서명옥's 28-point margin in 갑 exceeds most uncompetitive races.

issues.label: Wealth / class composition
issues.description: Gangnam's concentration of high-income households is widely considered the defining voting driver — consistent across election cycles, largely insulating the district from national swings.

issues.label: Real-estate politics
issues.description: High-rise apartment density in 압구정, 청담, 역삼 correlates strongly with preference for pro-property-value, lower-tax candidates.

issues.label: Conservative identity
issues.description: Consistent PPP/conservative representation since 2000 (25+ years) suggests entrenched ideological alignment independent of any single cycle's issues.

issues.label: Structural conservative advantage
issues.description: Candidate selection, donor networks, and party organization all favor the conservative side here.
